signal_title: DOJ's Final Rule Implementing Executive Order 14117
risk_rationale: The new DOJ rule enforces prohibitions on data transfer to foreign adversaries, which may require immediate changes to TikTok's data transfer mechanisms to ensure compliance, particularly in the US market.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: EU-US transfers use EU-US Data Privacy Framework + SCCs.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Prohibitions on data transfer to foreign adversaries.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current baseline does not explicitly address prohibitions on data transfers to foreign adversaries, which may be required under the new DOJ rule.

remediations.title: Implement Data Transfer Restrictions to Foreign Adversaries
remediations.description: Develop and implement a mechanism to ensure that data transfers do not occur to entities identified as foreign adversaries under the new DOJ rule.
acceptance_criteria: Data transfers to foreign adversaries are blocked
Compliance with DOJ's Final Rule is documented

business_impact: Failure to comply with the DOJ rule could result in significant legal and financial penalties, affecting TikTok's operations in the US market.
recommended_actions: Review and update data transfer policies to comply with DOJ rule
Implement technical measures to block data transfers to foreign adversaries
Engage with legal counsel to ensure ongoing compliance with US regulations
